sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 10, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 9 of about 28 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good, LEFT undid the RIGHT. Now the right green frame is back at cols 27-29 with white center (active). Let me plan the full sequence:
Current state:
- Left frame: rows 30-32, cols 18-20, center color=c (charcoal, not active)
- Right frame: rows 30-32, cols 27-29, center color=W (white, active)
Targets:
- Left target: rows 32-36, cols 11-15 (charcoal 5x5 frame)
- Right target: rows 26-30, cols 44-48 (charcoal 5x5 frame)
For the right frame (active) to reach the right target:
- Current center: [31, 28]
- Target center: [28, 46] (center of 5x5 frame at rows 26-30, cols 44-48)
- Need: UP 1 (3 rows), RIGHT 5 (15 cols)
- But wait, the purple barrier is in the way. Let me check where it is.
The purple bar is between the two white panels. Looking at the image, it seems to be around cols 33-35 or so. The right frame needs to pass through or around it.
Actually, looking more carefully at the layout, the right target is in the right white panel, and the right green frame starts in the left white panel. The purple bar separates them. The frame might need to go around the barrier.
Let me first try moving the right frame UP and RIGHT to see if it can pass through the barrier. If not, I'll need to find another path.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The right target is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. The right green frame is at rows 30-32, cols 27-29. To get to the target:
- UP 1: rows 27-29
- RIGHT 5: cols 27+15=42 to 44-48... wait, let me recalculate.
Frame is 3x3, so it spans 3 rows and 3 cols. Current: rows 30-32, cols 27-29.
After UP 1: rows 27-29, cols 27-29
After RIGHT 5: rows 27-29, cols 27+15=42 to 44... hmm, 27+15=42, so cols 42-44.
But the target is at cols 44-48. So the frame would be at cols 42-44, not centered in the target.
Wait, let me recalculate. The target is a 5x5 frame at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. The center of this frame is at row 28, col 46. For the 3x3 green frame to be centered inside, its center should also be at row 28, col 46.
Current right frame center: row 31, col 28
Target center: row 28, col 46
Difference: row -3 (UP 1), col +18 (RIGHT 6)
So I need UP 1, RIGHT 6. Let me try that.
But first, I need to check if the frame can pass through the purple barrier. Let me just try and see.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state and plan movement
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
n_frames = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']

# Find active frame (has W center)
for n in n_frames:
    for cid in n['children']:
        child = next(x for x in nodes if x['id'] == cid)
        if child['color'] == 'W':
            print(f"Active frame: boundary={n['boundary']}, center at {child['boundary']}")

# Find targets
c_frames = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 16]
print("\nCharcoal targets:")
for n in c_frames:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Plan: move active (right) frame to right target
# Active frame center: [31, 28], target center: [28, 46]
# Need UP 1, RIGHT 6
# Try UP first then RIGHT
action(['UP', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves active green frame right by 3 cols VERIFIED", "LEFT": "Moves active green frame left by 3 cols VERIFIED", "UP": "Moves active green frame up by 3 rows VERIFIED", "DOWN": "Moves active green frame down by 3 rows VERIFIED", "MOUSE": "Clicking on green frame selects it (gives white center) VERIFIED"}, "object_taxonomy": {"g": "gray background", "w": "white panels/squares", "p": "purple vertical bar", "N": "light green 3x3 frames (movable tokens)", "c": "charcoal 5x5 frames (targets)", "W": "white single pixel (selected token center)", "B": "black single pixel (unselected token center)"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "row 63, charcoal strip"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Right frame active. Need to move it to right target at rows 26-30, cols 44-48.", "goal_model": "Move green frames into charcoal target frames", "recent_findings": ["MOUSE selects active frame", "Arrow keys move active frame by 3 pixels"], "open_questions": ["Can frames pass through purple barrier?"], "current_plan": "Move right frame UP 1, RIGHT 6 to reach right target", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
